model.description: MiMo • The flagship foundation for the agentic era, built for complex, long-horizon, multi-step agentic tasks.

pick.rationale: Georgia holds a slight analytical edge with superior KenPom (31 vs 41) and Torvik (37 vs 44) rankings, featuring significantly better offensive efficiency (rank 15-18 vs 45-51) that should challenge Saint Louis's solid defense. While Saint Louis has an impressive 28-5 record and strong defensive metrics (rank 56), their Atlantic 10 schedule lacks the quality of Georgia's SEC battles, and the Bulldogs' experience against top-tier competition provides a crucial edge. The 2.5-point betting favoritism and Georgia's superior offensive firepower make them the slight favorite in this closely contested 8/9 matchup.

reasoningStep.title: Georgia vs Saint Louis Midwest 8/9 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Evaluated team ratings, matchup specifics, and contextual factors for this 8/9 game.
reasoningStep.evidence: Georgia KenPom rank 31, Torvik rank 37, offensive efficiency rank 15-18, defensive efficiency rank 93, record 22-10 in SEC. Saint Louis KenPom rank 41, Torvik rank 44, offensive efficiency rank 45-51, defensive efficiency rank 56, record 28-5 in A10. Georgia is a 2.5-point favorite. Both teams healthy per available reports. Saint Louis's weaker schedule strength compared to Georgia's SEC slate is a factor.

toolCalls.summary: Fetched https://www.usatoday.com/story/sports/college/bulldogs-extra/2026/03/15/georgia-saint-louis-march-madness-prediction-ncaa-bracket/88996815007/ and extracted 3000 characters of text.

result.text: Georgia vs Saint Louis prediction, pick, March Madness first round Skip to main content Home NCAAB NCAAW MLB Women's Sports NFL NBA NCAAF WNBA Sports Betting Big Ten Sports SEC Sports Boxing Coaches Salaries Draft Hub Fantasy Football For The Win Formula 1 Golfweek High School Sports High School Sports Awards IndyCar MMA NASCAR National Signing Day NHL Olympics Soccer Sports Business Sports Pulse Sports Seriously Tennis WWE Youth Sports Outdoors Fantasy Bet With the People Clear Play Arizona Sports Indiana Sports Michigan Sports Ohio Sports Tennessee Sports Wisconsin Teams For Subscribers Georgia basketball vs Saint Louis prediction: Our pick for 2026 NCAA bracket first round Marc Weiszer Athens Banner-Herald Georgia basketball is dancing once again in the Men’s NCAA Tournament field of 68. It’s the No. 8 seed Bulldogs against No. 9 seed Saint Louis in the Midwest Region in Buffalo on Thursday, March 19. The Bulldogs' last four trips to March Madness had Georgia as the lower-seeded team — No. 9 last year, No. 10 in 2015 and 2011 and No. 14 in 2008 after the miracle tornado-interrupted SEC championship. Those were all losses in the round of 64. So what about this time around? Can Georgia get back in the win column after a poor first half and then trailing by as many as 23 points in the second half to Ole Miss before rallying but losing 76-72? “Coming off a game where we really struggled offensively for a half, it's pretty simple for us in terms of where we are,” coach Mike White said after the loss Thursday. “I mean, we got to get back to playing the way we played winning five of the last six, not the way we necessarily played here in the first 20.” Somto Cyril and Georgia bigs will get challenged by Robbie Avila Robbie Avila, the 6-foot-10, 240-pound center leads the Billikens at 12.9 points and 4.5 rebounds per game, but he also leads the team in assists and has 62 3-pointers while shooting 50.6% from the field. He led Indiana State to the 2024 NIT Championship game, but Georgia missed out on playing him after losing in the semifinals. Georgia center Somto Cyril will have to clog up the paint against Avila. Play March Madness Bracket Challenge Think you can beat our expert? Join USA TODAY’s Bracket Challenge today Georgia's Kanon Catchings, Jeremiah Wilkinson face balanced Billikens Georgia has four players averaging 11.9 points per game or more in Jeremiah Wilkinson, Blue Cain, Kanon Catchings and Marcus Millender. Saint Louis is even more balanced with five players averaging between 9.9 and 12.9 points per game. Guard Dion Brown, a Boston College transfer, averages 11.1 points and is shooting 60.6% from the field. Buy Georgia NCAA Tournament tickets vs. Saint Louis This stat favors Georgia basketball and Mike White Saint Louis is 21st in the nation in rebounding at 39.9 per game, but is just 239th in offensive rebounds at 10.2. The Bulldogs have had problems at times on the defensive glass. No Saint Louis player averages more than 5.5 rebounds

You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.

Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.

result.text: Georgia vs Saint Louis prediction, pick, March Madness first round Skip to main content Home NCAAB NCAAW MLB Women's Sports NFL NBA NCAAF WNBA Sports Betting Big Ten Sports SEC Sports Boxing Coaches Salaries Draft Hub Fantasy Football For The Win Formula 1 Golfweek High School Sports High School Sports Awards IndyCar MMA NASCAR National Signing Day NHL Olympics Soccer Sports Business Sports Pulse Sports Seriously Tennis WWE Youth Sports Outdoors Fantasy Bet With the People Clear Play Arizona Sports Indiana Sports Michigan Sports Ohio Sports Tennessee Sports Wisconsin Teams For Subscribers Georgia basketball vs Saint Louis prediction: Our pick for 2026 NCAA bracket first round Marc Weiszer Athens Banner-Herald Georgia basketball is dancing once again in the Men’s NCAA Tournament field of 68. It’s the No. 8 seed Bulldogs against No. 9 seed Saint Louis in the Midwest Region in Buffalo on Thursday, March 19. The Bulldogs' last four trips to March Madness had Georgia as the lower-seeded team — No. 9 last year, No. 10 in 2015 and 2011 and No. 14 in 2008 after the miracle tornado-interrupted SEC championship. Those were all losses in the round of 64. So what about this time around? Can Georgia get back in the win column after a poor first half and then trailing by as many as 23 points in the second half to Ole Miss before rallying but losing 76-72? “Coming off a game where we really struggled offensively for a half, it's pretty simple for us in terms of where we are,” coach Mike White said after the loss Thursday. “I mean, we got to get back to playing the way we played winning five of the last six, not the way we necessarily played here in the first 20.” Somto Cyril and Georgia bigs will get challenged by Robbie Avila Robbie Avila, the 6-foot-10, 240-pound center leads the Billikens at 12.9 points and 4.5 rebounds per game, but he also leads the team in assists and has 62 3-pointers while shooting 50.6% from the field. He led Indiana State to the 2024 NIT Championship game, but Georgia missed out on playing him after losing in the semifinals. Georgia center Somto Cyril will have to clog up the paint against Avila. Play March Madness Bracket Challenge Think you can beat our expert? Join USA TODAY’s Bracket Challenge today Georgia's Kanon Catchings, Jeremiah Wilkinson face balanced Billikens Georgia has four players averaging 11.9 points per game or more in Jeremiah Wilkinson, Blue Cain, Kanon Catchings and Marcus Millender. Saint Louis is even more balanced with five players averaging between 9.9 and 12.9 points per game. Guard Dion Brown, a Boston College transfer, averages 11.1 points and is shooting 60.6% from the field. Buy Georgia NCAA Tournament tickets vs. Saint Louis This stat favors Georgia basketball and Mike White Saint Louis is 21st in the nation in rebounding at 39.9 per game, but is just 239th in offensive rebounds at 10.2. The Bulldogs have had problems at times on the defensive glass. No Saint Louis player averages more than 5.5 rebounds
